title: Quetiapine to Reduce Post Concussive Syndrome After Mild Traumatic Brain Injury (mTBI)

skipped_criteria: Inclusion 1: Veteran status cannot be expressed with available schema fields.

Inclusion 2: mTBI diagnosis specifics (loss of consciousness, altered consciousness, posttraumatic amnesia), PCS symptoms on NSI, and requirement that 6+ months elapsed since injury cannot be expressed with available schema fields.
Inclusion 3: Requirement of being stable on at least three CNS active psychotropic medications cannot be expressed with available schema fields.
Inclusion 4: PTSD Checklist for DSM-5 (PCL-5) score ≥25 cannot be expressed with available schema fields.
Exclusion 1: Moderate or severe TBI or major neurocognitive disorder (dementia) cannot be distinguished from mild TBI using available schema fields.
Exclusion 2: DSM-5 criteria for schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, schizoaffective disorder, or recent inpatient hospitalization cannot be expressed with available schema fields.
Exclusion 3: Current antipsychotic or prohibited medication use cannot be expressed with available schema fields.

Exclusion 4: Known intolerance to quetiapine or clinically unstable cardiac, pulmonary, hepatic, renal, hematological, endocrinological conditions, diabetes, severe sleep apnea, or seizure disorder cannot be expressed with available schema fields.
Exclusion 5: Substance use disorder requiring medication treatment or medical detoxification or inpatient hospitalization within 6 months cannot be expressed with available schema fields.
Exclusion 6: Suicidal ideation (CSSRS type 4 or 5), homicidal ideation with intent/plan, or suicidal behavior within specified timeframes cannot be expressed with available schema fields.
Exclusion 7: Cardiac arrhythmia or QTc interval ≥470 ms cannot be expressed with available schema fields.
Exclusion 8: Lactating women and those of childbearing potential not using reliable contraception cannot be fully expressed; only pregnant/unknown pregnancy status is encoded.
